name: Cao Zhao (Changsi)
page_title: Cao Zhao (Changsi) 曹肇 (長思) [Wei] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives

sections.short-biography: One of the possible regents for Cao Fang but outmaneuvered by Liu Fang and Sun Zi.

sections.biography-source-undefined:
Cao Zhao was styled Changsi. He inherited the title of Marquis of Changping from his father, Cao Xiu. Cao Zhao was said to be a man of considerable talent.

When Cao Rui was extremely sick, he wanted Cao Zhao to be one of the regents for Cao Fang, but changed his mind due to the words of Liu Fang and Sun Zi. Sima Yi was instead chosen to be a regent, and Cao Zhao strongly protested this appointment. Although he got Cao Rui to delay the appointment, he not only failed to fully convince Cao Rui, but was outmaneuvered by Liu Fang and Sun Zi, who convinced Cao Rui to remove Cao Zhao from the capital with the excuse that Cao Zhao should attend to his fiefdom at Changping as its marquis. Cao Zhao left in tears.

During the Zhengshi era (240–249), he died. He was posthumously made General of the Guards.

full_text:
Cao Zhao (Changsi) 曹肇 (長思)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Cao Zhao (曹肇), styledChangsi (長思). One of the possible regents for Cao Fang but outmaneuvered by Liu Fang and Sun Zi. Cao Zhao was affiliated with the Wei Kingdom.

Sima Yi was instead chosen to be a regent, and Cao Zhao strongly protested this appointment.
